purpose: Per-faction-pair relationship lattice for sere. Drives Layer 11 faction-strategy + cross-world relationship seeding. The 'managed_parity' edges encode the Tessera funding both sides of a war so it never resolves — the satire's central mechanic, discoverable via the Ledger lens.

edges.history: The Border Mirror. Two anchor-realms who were brothers under the Twin Pact until a manufactured incident in the contested passes sundered them. Sixty years of border hatred — lit, and kept lit.
edges.cross_world_resonance: The split-the-anchors pattern that crushed the Table-of-All; mirrors any engineered fraternal war.

edges.history: The Tessera supplies the Reach (through Verge) just enough to keep fighting and never enough to win — because a decisive victory would end the procurement.
edges.cross_world_resonance: Parity as product — the Long Mirror, miniaturized onto one front.

edges.history: The same crates that arm Dovrane arm Keshar. Sarn's dual manifests prove it. The balance is the point: a balanced war buys twice.
edges.cross_world_resonance: The mirror image of the Tessera→Dovrane edge — together they are the managed-parity web.

edges.history: Verge sells the Reach the spear and the shield, and bills it for the threat of Keshar.
Verge sells Keshar the same catalogue, and bills it for the threat of Dovrane. Its annual report calls the arrangement 'a stable demand environment.'
edges.cross_world_resonance: Fear as a market — the other half.

edges.history: Keshar is a creditor and cannot be trapped by a loan, so the Fund waits: when the export market closes and the border war can no longer be paid for, the rescue arrives with its conditions.
The Reach half-suspects the rescue is coming and dreads it more than the war.
Keshar still saves some of its own grain; Hollowford regards that as lost revenue and an example that must not spread.
The coalition the machine cannot allow to cohere. Every prior Open Table was bought, hollowed, or split; this one was designed around those exact deaths.
edges.cross_world_resonance: The Ninth Refusal trying to be born on the world that forecloses it — its survivors become the ark program.

edges.history: Dovrane is one of the two anchors the Open Table needs. Healing the Twin Pact is the precondition for critical mass.
Keshar is the other anchor — the larger productive base. With both, the Open Table is unstoppable; that is exactly why the Pact had to break.
The ledger and the tower that clears it — the still point that lets the wars below run forever.
Founded in the same season of the Quiet Fall; the Curtain keeps off the public record what Verge would rather not invoice in daylight.
edges.cross_world_resonance: Managing the seeable, not the secret.
